question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a crafted YAML/env-var configuration with a blank-space scenario name that passes the `StringUtils::isBlank` check but causes `scenarios.forEach((name, property) -> property.setName(name))` to assign a whitespace name, leading to silent metric label corruption and inability to distinguish scenario health during a network partition event?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a null key into the `scenarios` LinkedHashMap via environment variable binding, bypassing the `StringUtils::isBlank` null-check path and causing a NullPointerException in `property.setName(name)` that crashes the monitor during a network partition when recovery logic depends on scenario name resolution?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `enabled=true` and provide zero scenarios through a race condition in Spring's `@ConfigurationProperties` binding, causing `validate()` to throw `IllegalArgumentException` and halt the monitor process entirely during a network partition, preventing any recovery or alerting?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: nodeMaxBackoff] Can an unprivileged external user set `hiero.mirror.monitor.publish.nodeMaxBackoff` to an extremely large value (e.g., `Duration.ofDays(365)`) via environment variable, causing all SDK clients to back off indefinitely during a network partition and never attempt reconnection, resulting in a complete and permanent monitoring blackout?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: nodeMaxBackoff] Can an unprivileged external user set `nodeMaxBackoff` to the minimum allowed value (no `@DurationMin` constraint is present on this field) of zero or near-zero, causing the SDK client to spin-retry all partitioned nodes in a tight loop, exhausting CPU and network resources and amplifying the impact of a network partition into a full denial-of-service?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: nodeMaxBackoff] Can an unprivileged external user observe that `nodeMaxBackoff` has no `@DurationMin` constraint and set it to `PT0S` (zero duration), causing `client.setNodeMaxBackoff()` in `TransactionPublisher.toClient()` to configure zero backoff, resulting in unbounded retry storms against partitioned nodes that overwhelm remaining healthy nodes?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: responseThreads] Can an unprivileged external user set `responseThreads` to its minimum value of 1 (enforced by `@Min(1)`) via environment variable, causing all 40-default async response futures to queue on a single thread, and during a network partition where responses are delayed, causing the single thread to block indefinitely and starve all transaction acknowledgment processing?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: responseThreads] Can an unprivileged external user set `responseThreads` to an arbitrarily large integer (no `@Max` constraint exists) via environment variable, causing the JVM to spawn thousands of threads during a network partition recovery phase, exhausting thread pool resources and causing an OutOfMemoryError that crashes the monitor?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: clients] Can an unprivileged external user set `clients` to an extremely large value (no `@Max` constraint on `clients`) via environment variable, causing `TransactionPublisher.getClients()` to instantiate thousands of SDK `Client` objects each holding open gRPC connections, exhausting file descriptors and connection slots during a network partition when connections are not properly closed?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: clients] Can an unprivileged external user set `clients=1` (minimum allowed by `@Min(1)`) to eliminate round-robin distribution, causing all transactions during a network partition to be routed through a single SDK client, creating a single point of failure where one partitioned node blocks all publishing activity?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: warmupPeriod] Can an unprivileged external user set `warmupPeriod` to `PT0S` (zero, allowed by `@DurationMin(seconds = 0)`) via environment variable, causing the `CompositeTransactionGenerator` to skip rate ramp-up and immediately flood partitioned nodes at full TPS upon startup, amplifying the partition's impact by saturating remaining healthy nodes with burst traffic?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: warmupPeriod] Can an unprivileged external user set `warmupPeriod` to an extremely large value (no `@DurationMax` constraint) via environment variable, causing the `RateLimiter` in `CompositeTransactionGenerator` to ramp up so slowly that the monitor never reaches operational TPS, effectively silencing all transaction publishing during and after a network partition recovery?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: batchDivisor] Can an unprivileged external user set `batchDivisor` to exactly 100 (minimum allowed by `@Min(100)`) while configuring a scenario with very high TPS, causing `Math.ceil(total / batchDivisor)` in `CompositeTransactionGenerator.rebuild()` to produce an extremely large batch size that overwhelms the thread pool during a network partition when responses are slow?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: batchDivisor] Can an unprivileged external user set `batchDivisor` to `Integer.MAX_VALUE` (no `@Max` constraint) via environment variable, causing `Math.ceil(total / batchDivisor)` to round down to zero and `Math.max(1, 0)` to produce a batch size of 1, effectively throttling the monitor to 1 transaction per rate-limiter tick and masking a network partition by making the monitor appear healthy at near-zero throughput?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: statusFrequency] Can an unprivileged external user set `statusFrequency` to its minimum of 1 second (enforced by `@DurationMin(seconds = 1L)`) via environment variable, causing the monitor to log publishing statistics every second and flood log storage during a network partition when error rates spike, potentially filling disk and causing secondary failures?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: statusFrequency] Can an unprivileged external user set `statusFrequency` to an extremely large value (no `@DurationMax` constraint) via environment variable, causing the monitor to never log publishing statistics during a network partition, preventing operators from detecting the partition through log-based alerting?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: async] Can an unprivileged external user set `async=false` via environment variable, causing `TransactionPublisher.execute()` to use synchronous `executable.execute(client)` wrapped in `Mono.fromCallable()`, which blocks `responseThreads` threads during a network partition when nodes are unresponsive, leading to thread pool exhaustion and complete monitor starvation?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: async] Can an unprivileged external user toggle `async=true` (default) to exploit the fact that async futures accumulate unboundedly in the `responseThreads` pool during a network partition, causing heap exhaustion from queued `CompletableFuture` objects that never complete because partitioned nodes never respond?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: scenarios] Can an unprivileged external user inject an extremely large number of scenario entries into the `scenarios` LinkedHashMap via environment variable array binding, causing `validate()` to iterate over thousands of entries and `CompositeTransactionGenerator` to instantiate thousands of `ConfigurableTransactionGenerator` objects, exhausting heap memory before a network partition is even detected?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: scenarios] Can an unprivileged external user craft scenario names containing special characters (e.g., `{`, `}`, `$`, `#`) that are valid non-blank strings passing `StringUtils::isBlank`, but cause metric label injection in Micrometer tags used by `PublishMetrics`, corrupting monitoring dashboards and masking network partition events from operators?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: scenarios] Can an unprivileged external user set a scenario name to an extremely long string (no length constraint in `validate()`), causing `property.setName(name)` to assign an unbounded string that is later used as a Micrometer tag value, potentially causing tag cardinality explosion in the metrics registry and memory exhaustion during a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `validate()` is annotated `@PostConstruct` and runs only once at startup, meaning that dynamically reloaded configuration (e.g., via Spring Cloud Config refresh) bypasses `validate()`, allowing injection of blank scenario names or null values that corrupt the `scenarios` map during a live network partition recovery?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: nodeMaxBackoff] Can an unprivileged external user set `nodeMaxBackoff` to a negative `Duration` (no `@DurationMin` constraint), causing `client.setNodeMaxBackoff()` in `TransactionPublisher.toClient()` to pass a negative duration to the Hedera SDK, triggering undefined SDK behavior that may disable backoff entirely and cause retry storms against partitioned nodes?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: clients] Can an unprivileged external user set `clients` to a value larger than the number of available consensus nodes, causing `TransactionPublisher.getClients()` to create more SDK clients than there are nodes, and during a network partition where some nodes are unreachable, the `secureRandom.nextInt(publishProperties.getClients())` index selection may repeatedly select clients bound to partitioned nodes, causing disproportionate failure rates?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Field: responseThreads] Can an unprivileged external user set `responseThreads` to a value that exactly matches the number of in-flight transactions during a network partition, causing all threads to block on unresponsive node responses and leaving zero threads available to process responses from healthy nodes, creating a complete processing deadlock?
